strategy_summary:
Naisora's SEO strategy focuses on owning the 'restaurant website Bangalore' keyword cluster — a niche with low competition and high buyer intent. The first 3 weeks should target Quick Win keywords (pricing, Zomato comparison, Google visibility) that have almost zero competition from big brands and can rank within 30 days. These keywords match the exact pain points of Bangalore restaurant owners: they're paying too much to Zomato, not showing on Google, and don't know what a website costs.

Weeks 4-7 expand into problem-solving content (getting more customers, direct orders without aggregators, Google Maps ranking) which captures higher search volume and positions Naisora as the authority on restaurant digital growth. The final weeks target the core money keywords (restaurant/cafe website design Bangalore) which will rank faster because the earlier content has already built topical authority.

Critical success factors: Every blog must follow the 7-part conversion structure (Hook → Problem → Impact → Solution → Steps → Service Connection → CTA), mention local Bangalore neighborhoods, and end with a clear call-to-action for a free website audit. The content ratio should be 80% client-generating (these 10 keywords) and 20% authority-building trend pieces.
